seoTitle: How to Read and Extract Text From a PDF in n8n
metaDescription: Learn how to use n8n's Extract From File node to pull text out of PDF documents and pass it downstream as structured data.
intro: Extracting text from PDFs inside n8n turns unstructured documents into data your workflow can actually use, whether for search, summarization, or routing. This guide covers bringing in the PDF, running it through the Extract From File node, and confirming the extracted text looks right before sending it downstream.

steps.heading: Bring the PDF into your workflow
steps.body: Fetch the file via an HTTP Request node or receive it as binary data from an upstream source.

steps.heading: Add the Extract From File node
steps.body: Place this node after your PDF source so it can process the binary content.

steps.heading: Set the operation to read text from PDF
steps.body: Configure the node to target your incoming binary property and extract readable text.

steps.heading: Choose your output style
steps.body: Decide whether you want the text merged into one string or split by page.

steps.heading: Review and route the output
steps.body: Check the extracted text in the preview pane, then connect it to your next logic steps.

faq.question: What node extracts text from a PDF in n8n?
faq.answer: The Extract From File node handles converting PDF binary data into readable text.

faq.question: Can I extract text page by page instead of one big string?
faq.answer: Yes, the node offers output options that let you split extracted text by page.

faq.question: Can I send the extracted PDF text to an AI model?
faq.answer: Yes, once extracted, the text can be routed downstream into any node, including AI models for summarization or analysis.

faq.question: Why isn't my PDF text extracting cleanly?
faq.answer: Formatting issues can happen with complex layouts, so check the binary property reference and the source file quality.
